project_reference.core_research_question: For adaptive gap-junction networks with bistable fast voltage dynamics and slow coupling plasticity, what mathematically controlled regime, if any, supports Lyapunov-like reduction, morphology-conditioned information retention, and falsifiable repair thresholds before nonconservative flux, disorder, topology change, or fast-slow breakdown destroy that description?

scope.question: What controlled regime, if any, supports Lyapunov-like structure, explicit generative correspondence, nonlinear repair reachability, and dissipation-bounded memory in adaptive gap-junction networks before nonconservative flux, disorder, topology change, or fast-slow breakdown invalidate that description?
in_scope: 2D square-lattice fast-slow stochastic model with adaptive gap-junction couplings
Topology-correct lesion comparisons across sink, edge-sever, and topological-cut classes
Explicit disorder classification and relevance testing
Ensemble-based nonlinear reachability, basin geometry, mutual-information decay, and entropy-production analysis
Three-valued WT/Cryptic/DH macrostate handling or an explicit binary-reduction limitation
out_of_scope: Inference or belief claims without explicit P(m) and P(V|m)
Higher-dimensional or long-range-coupling extensions before the nearest-neighbor base case is verified
unresolved_questions: Which local plasticity rule Phi(Delta V, s_ij, xi_ij) should be the base case?
Which coarse-grained voltage feature map phi(V) should define P(V|m) for WT, Cryptic, and DH?
What reduced-order patch is acceptable for quasipotential estimation if the full lattice is too expensive?

context_gaps: No external literature benchmarks loaded yet
No base-case plasticity rule selected yet
No final WT/Cryptic/DH representation selected yet

formulations: Fast voltage layer with nonlinear double-well or excitable on-site dynamics
Slow symmetric adaptive conductance layer in the base case
Empirical morphology macrostate WT/Cryptic/DH or an explicit binary-reduction limitation
forbidden_estimator_families: Linear Gramian as the primary reachability metric
allowed_fit_families: Finite-size scaling with explicit crossover-versus-transition classification
Asymptotic expansions in epsilon with reported breakdown thresholds
forbidden_fit_families: Post hoc variational-objective fitting presented as exact equivalence
stop_and_rethink_conditions: A reduced Lyapunov claim requires uncontrolled neglected terms
A lesion routine conflates sink behavior with topological cuts
Octanol disorder is modeled as uniform conductance scaling instead of bond dilution
A memory claim is made without a dissipation number
Inference language appears before explicit P(m) and P(V|m)

observables.name: Lyapunov-structure status
observables.definition: Whether the deterministic (V, G) dynamics admit a global, local, reduced, or no Lyapunov functional

observables.name: Fast-slow breakdown threshold
observables.definition: Largest epsilon or forcing amplitude for which the reduced description remains within tolerance

observables.name: Empirical nonlinear reachability
observables.definition: Ensemble-defined reachability statistic conditioned on attractor neighborhood or basin-sampling ensemble

observables.name: Morphological macrostate classification
observables.definition: Classification among WT, Cryptic, and DH, with DH treated as a stable non-equilibrium attractor

observables.name: Basin geometry
observables.definition: Quasipotential, minimum-action, or transition-path characterization of attractor basins

observables.name: Information decay length
observables.definition: Empirical spatial decay length extracted from injury-response or macrostate-feature mutual information

observables.name: Entropy production rate
observables.definition: Pathwise KL divergence rate between forward and time-reversed trajectory ensembles

claims.statement: The project defines the adaptive gap-junction model with topology-correct lesions, explicit disorder classes, and documented nondimensional controls.
The project proves, disproves, or brackets the regime of Lyapunov-like structure and reports obstruction terms and breakdown thresholds.
The project either represents the empirical WT/Cryptic/DH macrostate explicitly or labels any binary reduction as a scope limitation.
Empirical reachability, basin geometry, and information decay are measured separately and compared directly rather than inferred from linear surrogates.
Octanol-style blockade is modeled as bond dilution and the polarity-disruption threshold is tested against the 2D square-lattice bond-percolation point p_c ~ 0.5.
Morphological memory or repair-fidelity claims are bounded by measured entropy production from forward and reverse trajectory data.

deliverables.description: Python codebase for simulation, lesions, reachability, basin analysis, MI estimation, entropy production, and parameter sweeps
Phase 1 model definition and baseline simulation note
Derivation of Lyapunov structure, obstruction terms, and epsilon corrections
Ensemble-defined reachability, basin geometry, and information-decay comparison
Pathwise entropy-production analysis from forward and reverse trajectory ensembles
Required figure suite for threshold, reachability, basin, MI, entropy, and epsilon-regime results
Summary table listing claim, mathematical status, assumptions, disorder dependence, energetic cost, biological interpretation, and falsifiability

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-model-foundation
acceptance_tests.procedure: Audit Phase 1 artifacts to verify sink, edge-sever, and topological-cut lesions are separate implementations with graph surgery used only for topological cuts.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: All lesion classes are explicitly implemented and zero-out lesions are not presented as cuts.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-lyapunov-status
acceptance_tests.procedure: Review the derivation to confirm whether Lyapunov structure is proved, disproved, or bracketed, and verify that the conclusion is labeled by mathematical status.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The derivation gives a precise classification with explicit operating assumptions and no hidden global generalization.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-macrostate-handling
acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify that the project either implements WT/Cryptic/DH explicitly or marks any binary reduction as a scope limitation in the analysis artifacts.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No binary macrostate reduction is used silently.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-nonlinear-propagation
acceptance_tests.procedure: Check that Ensemble A and Ensemble B parameters are saved numerically and used in the reported reachability analysis.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: All reported reachability results have explicit ensemble metadata with initial-condition distribution, perturbation amplitude, duration, horizon, and trajectory count.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-nonlinear-propagation
acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify that basin claims are backed by quasipotential, minimum-action, or transition-path statistics rather than FTLEs alone.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No basin-boundary conclusion rests solely on FTLE calculations.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-bond-dilution-threshold
acceptance_tests.procedure: Compare the polarity-disruption threshold under bond dilution against connectivity and cluster-spanning observables and the 2D square-lattice bond-percolation point p_c ~ 0.5.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The project states whether the observed threshold coincides with, is dressed by, or is distinct from p_c ~ 0.5.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-thermodynamic-cost
acceptance_tests.procedure: Audit the entropy-production estimator to ensure forward and reverse path ensembles are defined and Delta t sensitivity is reported.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Entropy-production results include forward/reverse ensemble definitions, sampling interval, and a dissipation bound tied to any memory claim.

references.why_it_matters: Defines the mandatory model, verification, and interpretation constraints for the entire project.
Provides the project scaffold and workflow conventions used to organize the experiment.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-lyapunov-status
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Assuming joint (V, G) dynamics are globally gradient without proof
forbidden_proxies.reason: The core mathematical question is whether and where gradient structure fails.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-model-foundation
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Treating V_i = 0 node clamping as a topological cut
forbidden_proxies.reason: Zeroing creates a sink rather than removing graph connectivity.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-macrostate-handling
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Collapsing WT/Cryptic/DH to a binary macrostate without stating the loss explicitly
forbidden_proxies.reason: The empirical macrostate is three-valued and DH is not a transient.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-nonlinear-propagation
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Using a linear Gramian as the primary reachability metric
forbidden_proxies.reason: The voltage dynamics are nonlinear and bistable, so linear reachability is not the primary quantity.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-bond-dilution-threshold
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Modeling octanol blockade as uniform conductance scaling
forbidden_proxies.reason: The injected empirical constraint classifies octanol blockade as bond dilution.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-nonlinear-propagation
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Presenting FTLE ridges as basin boundaries
forbidden_proxies.reason: FTLEs indicate local sensitivity, not basin structure.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-thermodynamic-cost
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Claiming morphological memory without a dissipation estimate
forbidden_proxies.reason: The operator prompt requires a measured entropy-production number for memory claims.

weakest_anchors: No external literature benchmark has been loaded yet for disorder relevance, quasipotential practice, or entropy-production comparison
unvalidated_assumptions: A tractable explicit WT/Cryptic/DH representation can be implemented without overfitting the on-site potential
A reduced-order patch can preserve the basin geometry relevant to the full lattice
competing_explanations: The repair threshold may be a dressed percolation/connectivity transition rather than a distinct adaptive phenomenon
The WT/Cryptic/DH classification may require hidden slow variables beyond voltage alone
disconfirming_observations: Reachability, basin geometry, and information decay do not co-vary across the same parameter sweeps
Reduced Lyapunov structure breaks down before the regime where simulated repair signatures appear
Bond-dilution threshold does not track the expected p_c ~ 0.5 comparison in any clear way

position.last_activity_desc: Bootstrapped the project workspace and injected empirical constraints on timescales, macrostate structure, and bond dilution.

open_questions: Which local plasticity rule Phi(Delta V, s_ij, xi_ij) should define the base case?
Which coarse-grained feature map phi(V) is expressive enough for P(V|m) without post hoc fitting and while separating WT, Cryptic, and DH?
What reduced patch size is acceptable for basin-geometry estimation if the full lattice is too expensive?

decisions.summary: Use the supplied operator prompt as the governing scoping contract.
decisions.rationale: It contains the strongest domain constraints and explicit success criteria.
Avoid inventing benchmark references before they are actually loaded and checked.

convention_lock.natural_units: Lattice units with nondimensional control parameters
convention_lock.spin_basis: Binary head-tail polarity macrostate when needed
convention_lock.index_positioning: Sites i,j and edges (i,j) on a square lattice
convention_lock.time_ordering: Forward and time-reversed path ensembles for thermodynamic estimators

custom_conventions.lesion_convention: Sink, edge-sever, and topological cut are distinct classes
custom_conventions.basin_convention: FTLE is not a basin-boundary estimator
custom_conventions.result_status_convention: All conclusions labeled by mathematical status

blockers: No external literature benchmarks loaded yet
Base-case plasticity rule not selected yet
Coarse-grained feature map phi(V) not selected yet

session.stopped_at: Project bootstrap complete; Phase 1 ready to plan
